_source: FinCEN, FINTRAC, FCA, EBA, and respective national AML/CTF guidance. Thresholds current as of 2025. For synthetic data generation purposes only.
_notes: These thresholds are publicly known and criminal organizations design their operations explicitly around them. Structuring (breaking transactions to stay below reporting thresholds) is itself a federal crime in the US and equivalent offences exist in all listed jurisdictions.

US.CTR_description: Currency Transaction Report — mandatory filing for cash transactions exceeding $10,000 in a single day, by any combination of transactions. Filed by financial institutions with FinCEN.
US.structuring_law: 31 U.S.C. § 5324 — it is a federal crime to structure transactions specifically to avoid the $10,000 reporting requirement, even if the underlying funds are legitimate.
US.SAR_description: Suspicious Activity Report — mandatory filing for transactions of $5,000+ that the institution knows, suspects, or has reason to suspect involve illicit funds or have no lawful purpose. No minimum for transactions involving known bad actors.
US.BSA_recordkeeping_description: Banks must keep records of cash purchases of monetary instruments ($3,000–$10,000). The Bank Secrecy Act also requires records for wire transfers of $3,000+.
US.CMIR_description: Currency or Monetary Instrument Report — required when transporting >$10,000 cash or monetary instruments across US borders.
US.beneficial_ownership_description: FinCEN Customer Due Diligence rule: institutions must identify natural persons owning 25%+ of legal entity customers.

CA.LCTR_description: Large Cash Transaction Report — mandatory for cash transactions of CAD $10,000+ in a single transaction or multiple related transactions within 24 hours.
CA.EFTR_description: Electronic Funds Transfer Report — mandatory for international EFTs of CAD $10,000+ sent or received. Covers SWIFT, ACH, and Interac cross-border.
CA.STR_description: Suspicious Transaction Report — no minimum threshold; must be filed when reasonable grounds exist to suspect money laundering or terrorist financing.
CA.FINTRAC_24hr_description: Multiple transactions by or on behalf of the same individual totaling CAD $10,000+ within 24 hours must be treated as a single reportable transaction.

UK.SAR_description: Suspicious Activity Report — no minimum threshold in the UK. Any suspicion of money laundering triggers a mandatory reporting obligation under the Proceeds of Crime Act 2002 (POCA). Failure to report is a criminal offence.
UK.DAML_description: Defence Against Money Laundering — consent request sent to the NCA (National Crime Agency) before processing a transaction believed to involve criminal property. Provides legal protection if NCA consents.
UK.NCA_moratorium_description: NCA has 7 working days to respond to a DAML request; can extend to 31 days. Transaction must be paused during moratorium.
UK.cash_payment_limit_description: From January 2024: cash payments above £10,000 for goods are prohibited under UK AMLD implementation.

EU.AMLD_description: EU Anti-Money Laundering Directive (6AMLD) — cash transactions of €10,000+ trigger enhanced due diligence obligations for obligated entities.
EU.cash_payment_limit_description: EU Regulation 2024/1624 limits cash payments to €10,000 across the EU from 2027. Several member states have lower limits already (e.g. France €1,000 for professionals).
EU.wire_transfer_regulation: Regulation (EU) 2015/847 — complete payer and payee information required on all wire transfers. Missing information triggers enhanced scrutiny.
EU.STR_description: Suspicious Transaction Report — threshold varies by member state but 6AMLD mandates reporting of any suspicious activity regardless of amount.
EU.UBO_register_description: All EU member states must maintain Ultimate Beneficial Owner registers accessible to authorities and, in most cases, the public.

AU.TTR_description: Threshold Transaction Report — AUSTRAC requires reporting of cash transactions AUD $10,000+.
AU.SMR_description: Suspicious Matter Report — no minimum threshold; filed with AUSTRAC for any suspicious matter.
AU.IFTIe_description: International Funds Transfer Instructions — ALL international transfers (any amount) must be reported to AUSTRAC regardless of value.
AU.digital_currency_exchanges: Australian DCEs are reporting entities under AML/CTF Act — must file TTRs and SMRs same as banks.

SG.CTR_description: Cash Transaction Report — MAS requires filing for cash transactions SGD $20,000+.
SG.STR_description: Suspicious Transaction Report — no minimum; Suspicious Transaction Reporting Office (STRO) receives reports under the Corruption, Drug Trafficking and Other Serious Crimes Act.
SG.notes: Singapore is a major financial hub with significant cross-border flows. Gateway jurisdiction for Southeast Asian and China-linked fraud networks.

HK.DTCR_description: Cash reporting threshold approximately HKD $120,000 (≈ USD $15,000). JFIU (Joint Financial Intelligence Unit) receives STRs.
HK.notes: HKMA supervised institutions. High-volume cross-border flows with mainland China complicate tracing. Common transit jurisdiction for APAC fraud networks.
